about.title: About the Association
about.label: About
about.hero_subtitle: The Georgian Cartographers Association (GCA) is a professional and scientific organization, founded in 1998 in Georgia for the advancement of cartography, geodesy, and geoinformation sciences.
about.mission_text: The mission of the Georgian Cartographers Association is to promote the development of cartography, geographic information systems and geodesy, advance modern spatial technologies, support scientific and educational activities, and popularize cartographic knowledge in both professional and public spheres.
about.vision_text: The Association's vision is for Georgia to develop a modern, internationally-standardized cartographic and geoinformation environment that will support the advancement of science, education, public administration, and practical fields.
about.values_text: The Georgian Cartographers Association's work is grounded in professionalism, scientific accuracy, support for innovation, knowledge sharing, international cooperation, and developing the next generation of specialists.
about.history_1998: The Georgian Cartographers Association (GCA) was founded; Professor Jansug Kekelia was elected as the first president of the Association.
about.history_2006: By decision of the GCA Scientific Council, the collection "Cartography and Modernity" was published, dedicated to the 90th anniversary of the birth of Professor Alexander Aslanikashvili.
about.history_2014: Tengiz Gordeziani was elected Acting President of the GCA.
about.history_2015: On the initiative of the GCA, a scientific conference titled "Cartography. Perspectives for the Future" was held at TSU, dedicated to the 80th anniversary of Professor Jansug Kekelia.
about.history_2017: The GCA became one of the initiators of the international scientific conference "Landscape Dimension: Research, Planning, Management," dedicated to the 70th anniversary of Professor Niko Beruchashvili; in the same year, GCA members participated in an international scientific conference at Lviv University (Ukraine).
about.history_2018: GCA members participated in the international conference InterCarto/InterGIS-24 held under the auspices of the ICA (Bonn, Germany).
about.history_2019: GCA members participated in an international scientific conference held at Lviv University (Ukraine).
about.history_2020: On the initiative of the GCA and under the auspices of the ICA, the international conference InterCarto/InterGIS-26 was held online and a collection of conference materials was published.
about.history_2022: The GCA was one of the initiators of the international scientific conference "Landscape Dimension: Research, GIS, Planning, Management," dedicated to the 75th anniversary of Professor Niko Beruchashvili.
about.history_2023: GCA members participated in the ICA International Symposium "Atlases: Their Design and Use" (Czech Republic) and held official meetings with the Department of Geography at Masaryk University; in the same year, for the first time in the history of Georgian cartography, they participated in the ICA General Assembly and International Cartographic Conference (Cape Town, South Africa).
about.history_2024: The Georgian Cartographers Association became a member of the International Association of Geodesy and an affiliated member of the ICA; GCA members participated in international conferences "Cartography and Geoinformation Systems" (Nessebar, Bulgaria) and EuroCarto-24 (Vienna, Austria).
about.history_2025: The 5th Congress of the GCA was held, resulting in the election of Tengiz Gordeziani as President of the Association; GCA members participated in international conferences in Switzerland and Croatia; the GCA's English-language electronic journal "Kartografia" was established, and on the initiative of GCA members, the 12th issue of the "Caucasus Geographical Journal" was published with a section on "Cartography and Geoinformatics".
